content:
Supplier Spotlight: LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS) and Hyndburn Council

Key Findings

  • Hyndburn Borough Council spent a total of £211.3M across 29,804 transactions over ten financial years.
  • The largest single recipient of Hyndburn Borough Council funds was LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS), receiving £72.6M.
  • Payments to LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS) accounted for 34.4% of Hyndburn Borough Council's total analysed spending.
  • The top five suppliers combined received £122.7M, representing 58.1% of the council's total expenditure.
  • Suppliers outside the top ten accounted for £73.2M, or 34.6% of the total spend.

Hyndburn Borough Council's Spending Landscape

Hyndburn Borough Council's spending records, covering the financial years 2016/17 to 2025/26, reveal a total expenditure of £211.3M. This significant sum was distributed across 29,804 individual transactions. The analysis provides a comprehensive look at how public funds are allocated by the district council over a decade, highlighting key relationships and major service providers.

A notable characteristic of Hyndburn's spending is the concentration of funds among a relatively small number of suppliers. The top five suppliers alone received a combined total of £122.7M, which accounts for 58.1% of the council's entire analysed spend. Expanding this view, the top ten suppliers together received £138.1M, representing 65.4% of the total expenditure. This indicates that a substantial portion of the council's budget is directed towards a limited group of major service providers and statutory bodies, with the remaining £73.2M (34.6%) distributed among all other suppliers.

The Dominance of Lancashire County Council Payments

The most significant recipient of funds from Hyndburn Borough Council is LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS). This entity received a substantial £72.6M, making up 34.4% of Hyndburn's total analysed spending. This figure underscores the close financial relationship between the district council and the county council, which typically provides a range of services that district councils contribute towards, such as social care, education, and highways, often through direct debit arrangements.

Beyond the primary recipient, other organisations also received considerable sums. The (DD) DEPARTMENT OF LEVELLING UP, HOUSING & COMM.. was the second largest recipient, with payments totalling £22.7M, or 10.7% of the total spend. This suggests significant financial interactions related to housing and community initiatives, likely involving statutory payments or grants. ALLIANCE LEISURE SERVICES LTD ranked third, receiving £12.6M, which is 6.0% of the council's expenditure, indicating a substantial investment in leisure facilities or services within the borough.

Key Suppliers and Service Provision

A detailed breakdown of Hyndburn Borough Council's top suppliers reveals the diverse range of services and obligations the council manages. The table below outlines the ten largest recipients of council funds, demonstrating the scale of their financial engagement with Hyndburn.

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total
1 LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS) £72.6M 34.4%
2 (DD) DEPARTMENT OF LEVELLING UP, HOUSING & COMM.. £22.7M 10.7%
3 ALLIANCE LEISURE SERVICES LTD £12.6M 6.0%
4 (DD ONLY) RIBBLE VALLEY BOROUGH COUNCIL £7.9M 3.8%
5 MORGAN SINDALL CONSTRUCTION & INFRASTRUCTURE LTD £6.8M 3.2%
6 HYNDBURN LEISURE £5.6M 2.7%
7 HM REVENUE & CUSTOMS (DIRECT DEBIT) £3.5M 1.7%
8 NATIONAL HIGHWAYS LTD £2.2M 1.0%
9 ROSS LEE CONSTRUCTION LTD £2.2M 1.0%
10 SPECIALIST FLEET SERVICES LTD £1.9M 0.9%

The list includes other local government bodies, such as (DD ONLY) RIBBLE VALLEY BOROUGH COUNCIL, which received £7.9M (3.8%). This could indicate shared services or joint initiatives between neighbouring councils. Construction and infrastructure are also significant areas of expenditure, with MORGAN SINDALL CONSTRUCTION & INFRASTRUCTURE LTD receiving £6.8M (3.2%) and ROSS LEE CONSTRUCTION LTD receiving £2.2M (1.0%). These figures highlight the council's investment in maintaining and developing local infrastructure. Furthermore, payments to HYNDBURN LEISURE, amounting to £5.6M (2.7%), underscore the council's commitment to local leisure provision, potentially through a direct arm's-length organisation.

Statutory payments are also visible, with HM REVENUE & CUSTOMS (DIRECT DEBIT) receiving £3.5M (1.7%), representing tax and national insurance contributions. NATIONAL HIGHWAYS LTD received £2.2M (1.0%), indicating contributions or payments related to major road networks. Finally, SPECIALIST FLEET SERVICES LTD received £1.9M (0.9%), suggesting significant expenditure on vehicle maintenance or fleet management for council services.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Hyndburn Borough Council about the specific services and agreements funded by the £72.6M in payments to LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS).
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions on council spending and service provision.
  • Check Hyndburn Borough Council's published contracts register to understand the terms and scope of agreements with major suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor about the significant proportion of spending with a single supplier, LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL (DIRECT DEBIT PAYMENTS), and ask for more details on the value received for these payments.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Hyndburn Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
